stem: Let \( p(x) = x^{3} + ([[a]])x^{2} + ([[b]])x + ([[c]]) \). What is the remainder when \( p(x) \) is divided by \( x - ([[r]]) \)?
solution: By the Remainder Theorem, the remainder on division by \( x - r \) is \( p(r) \). Here \( p([[r]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This evaluates \( p(-r) \), the remainder for division by \( x + r \), not \( x - r \).
This drops the constant term \( c \) when evaluating \( p(r) \).
This drops the leading \( x^{3} \) term when evaluating \( p(r) \).
This is off by one from the correct remainder.
This is off by one from the correct remainder.
This is off by two from the correct remainder.

stem: For what value of \( k \) is \( x - ([[r]]) \) a factor of \( p(x) = x^{3} + ([[a]])x^{2} + kx + ([[c]]) \)?
solution: By the Factor Theorem, \( x - ([[r]]) \) is a factor exactly when \( p([[r]]) = 0 \): \( ([[r]])^{3} + ([[a]])([[r]])^{2} + k([[r]]) + ([[c]]) = 0 \), so \( k = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This solves for \( k \) but forgets to divide by \( r \), leaving the equation unsolved.
This has the correct terms but the wrong sign for \( k \cdot r \).
This drops the leading \( x^3 \) term's contribution \( r^3 \) before solving.
This is off by one from the correct value of \( k \).
This is off by one from the correct value of \( k \).
This is off by two from the correct value of \( k \).

stem: Let \( p(x) = x^{3} + ([[a]])x^{2} + ([[b]])x + ([[c]]) \). What is the remainder when \( p(x) \) is divided by \( [[m]]x - ([[n]]) \)?
solution: For a linear divisor \( mx - n \), the root is \( x = \dfrac{n}{m} \), and the generalized Remainder Theorem gives remainder \( p\!\left(\dfrac{[[n]]}{[[m]]}\right) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This evaluates \( p(n) \) instead of \( p(n/m) \), forgetting to divide by the leading coefficient \( m \).
This evaluates at the wrong sign, \( -n/m \), the root of \( mx+n \) instead of \( mx-n \).
This inverts the fraction, using \( m/n \) instead of \( n/m \) as the root.
This is off by one from the correct remainder.
This is off by one from the correct remainder.
This is off by two from the correct remainder.

stem: The remainder when \( p(x) = x^{3} + ([[a]])x^{2} + kx + ([[c]]) \) is divided by \( x - ([[r]]) \) is \( [[R]] \). What is \( k \)?
distractor_rationales: This forgets to divide by \( r \) after isolating the \( kr \) term.
This divides the whole remainder by \( r \) without first subtracting the other terms of \( p(r) \).
This has the correct magnitude but the wrong sign.
This is off by one from the correct value of \( k \).
This is off by one from the correct value of \( k \).
This is off by two from the correct value of \( k \).

stem: Let \( p(x) = x^{3} + ([[A]])x^{2} + ([[B]])x + ([[C]]) \). When \( p(x) \) is divided by \( (x-([[a]]))(x-([[b]])) \), the remainder has the form \( mx + n \). What is \( m \)?
solution: The remainder \( R(x) = mx+n \) must agree with \( p(x) \) at both roots of the divisor: \( R([[a]]) = p([[a]]) = [[pa]] \) and \( R([[b]]) = p([[b]]) = [[pb]] \). Solving this pair of equations for the slope gives \( m = \dfrac{[[pb]] - [[pa]]}{[[b]] - [[a]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to divide by the difference \( b-a \) between the two points.
This has the sign of the numerator reversed relative to the denominator.
This averages the two remainder values instead of taking the slope between them.
This is off by one from the correct slope.
This is off by one from the correct slope.
This is off by two from the correct slope.
